description: Fetch the Streptavidin-Biotin complex (PDB: 1STP) from RCSB into PyMOL. Identify the Biotin ligand (residue name BTN) and select the binding pocket (all protein residues within 4.0 Angstroms of the BTN ligand). Create a publication-quality visualization showing the pocket residues and ligand as sticks. Enable stereo rendering (e.g., using 'stereo cross_eyed' or 'stereo wall_eyed') and render a high-resolution PNG image of the stereo pair. Save the image to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/streptavidin_stereo.png. The image must have a wide aspect ratio (width/height >= 1.5) to accommodate the stereo view. Finally, write a structural report to ~/PyMOL_Data/streptavidin_pocket.txt containing: the PDB ID (1STP), the ligand name, and the specific residue numbers of at least three of the Tryptophan (Trp) residues that form the hydrophobic box lining the pocket (typically around positions 79, 92, 108, 120).
